summary: Ukrainian forces destroyed a Polish AHS Krab self-propelled howitzer in Kharkiv region using a fiber-optic FPV drone "Provod." Russian operators from the "Rubicon" Center reported 114 targets destroyed on March 9, 2026, across multiple directions including Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. A Russian soldier was hit by two Ukrainian FPV drones simultaneously while hiding behind a tree. Ukrainian experts are preparing to depart for the Middle East to train Gulf states in countering UAVs, following requests from several Middle Eastern nations. The enemy has significantly reduced BM-35 and BM-70 drone attacks on Sumy and other cities, likely due to production issues, increased electronic warfare, and the disconnection of Starlink systems.
